description.content:
Submits one or more Windows Autopatch tenant admin actions such as
`ClientBrokerInstall`. The call was recovered from the Tenant management bundle and
corroborated by a live install flow in the Intune admin center that changed the
`ClientBrokerInstall` row from `NotStarted` to `Pending`, then redirected the portal to
`ClientBroker.ReactView`.

The exact success status code and response body were not captured during the live flow;
after submission the portal polls `GET /tenant-management/v2/AdminActionsV/actionStatus`
for per-action state.

description.content:
The Intune Autopatch API is used by Windows Autopatch experiences inside the Microsoft
Intune admin center. The live surface is hosted on `services.autopatch.microsoft.com` and
backs blades such as:

  - Windows Autopatch > Autopatch groups
  - Windows Autopatch > Messages
  - Windows Autopatch > Support requests
  - Windows Autopatch > Tenant management
  - Roles > Windows Autopatch roles

Real tenant traffic confirmed the main tenant, reporting, support, and role-management
endpoints. A second pass mined the loaded `hosting.portal.azure.net/modernworkplace/*`
bundles for additional concrete GET paths under `/access-control/` and `/support/`, then
validated those with token-backed GET probes using the same browser session.

A related path, `GET /support/v1.0/Tenants/PreOnboardTenant`, returned `405 Method Not Allowed`
during probing and is therefore treated as a likely write-oriented onboarding flow rather than
documented as a confirmed GET operation here.

Requests use a browser-obtained bearer token together with portal headers such as
`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-client-session-id`, and `x-ms-effective-locale`.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best-efforts basis and may not be
100% accurate.
